item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was awesome but i think incident -3 should have come *after* the transcript

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
12/13/2020 Addendum-3 update. I've got a lot of things in the oven and I hope this update and playlist will give you some ideas of what's to come.

Hoping to get the next article up by next week. Thanks for your patience!

Updated Playlist Link

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hell of a first! We will be watching your career with great interest.

It has a healthy amount of weirdness, which I am always a fan of.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An amazingly weird article. I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is delightfully bizarre. +1'd with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and interesting, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I appreciate your support on it and I'm glad you like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much, I appreciate it. Also yes, Day Breaks is a huge influence on the creation of this idea. It's such an effective doomsday article for its visualization of the apocalypse the sun creates, I felt even someone trying to spoil it to the Foundation would find it hard to sell on words alone. So I'm glad a few people have said they picked up on the reference without any direct reference to it in the article itself, its how I wanted it to work.

In addition to other sun related articles listed in the piece itself and elsewhere on the site, I felt this one could easily slot in with any of those canons while remaining unqiue on its own and I'm glad to see it has accomplished that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It's fine, I just wanted to be clear it wasn't simply audio being played but a received signal of some kind. Though I see your point, I'll keep it mind for any future editing I need to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! I'm glad that came across. Seth obviously has some bizarre stances and morality but he does appreciate about those who support him, especially his "equipment." Still, I'm happy you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I originally didn't have one, but early advice helped me decide to get an image to set the tone early on. Happy to hear it helped you visualize.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Your assistance really helped to get the look for the article and the pacing just right so I'm happy it works better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you again for your help with it. Keeping it weird without being just confusing so the dialogue and pacing had to work. I'm glad I was able to pull it off for you.

item.title: Re: I like it.
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I appreciate the compliment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. You really feel for the host, his clear sincerity in the expression of his beliefs ensure that, bizarre as they are, you can take them as seriously as he does by the end of the piece. It's absurd, but it's not a joke. The apparent relationship between the host and the man is also great, it takes the bizarre hook of the anomaly (man with a radio in his belly) and ties it into the core narrative in an interesting way.

This is just really solid. It seems like it accomplishes what it's going for just about flawlessly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayyy, it got published!

Congrats on this one, Decoy. I enjoyed it in IRC and I'm enjoying it here as well. Great work, my guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great SCP! You've got some writing chops when it comes to the dialogue. The visual image of an old man with a radio antenna sticking out his abdomen is also pretty weird.

Eat the Sun before Day Breaks, everyone! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this on IRC, and it's great. I've still not completely sure the Foundation would use "radio broadcasts" for audio; but that's my inner ham speaking.
It was a really fun read, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a love dance between surrealism and absurdism, but it's more grounded in a way that I find nice. This is also a good example of a simple picture going a long way to setting tone; having a face to put to this radio message actually goes a really long way for me. And I must say, the picture you chose really fits the bill.

I am very happy to +1 this. And am doubly impressed that it's your first!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great to see this hit the mainsite, I loved it when I read it on IRC and it's even better now!

Eat the sun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a strange yet interesting scp. you got my upvote

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adored this when I critiqued it, and I do now. It’s a terrific first article. The dialogue is compelling, the anomaly is WEIRD, and the pacing is quite good.

Congrats

item.title: I like it.
item.content:encoded:
A unique idea for an SCP that has an interesting story to tell. That's pretty much it. I like this SCP a lot.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So many inspirations went into making this one, may make a longer post about the idea process behind this article if there's interest for it. It's my first so let me know what you liked or didn't like or any thoughts you might have!

Music Playlist

Special thanks goes out to these people for the critiques and advice:
Grigori Karpin WizzBlizz cybersqyd Nagiros DrSekora Cerastes and my friend [vampucci] who was essential in cleaning up my draft.

((If you messaged me over irc with critique and don't see your name PLEASE let me know so I can add you. It's my first so I really want to thank as many people who helped as possible. So thank you either way!))

Eat The Sun 🌞
